sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: Mastercard는 2025 회계연도에 327억 9,100만 달러의 매출과 149억 6,800만 달러의 순이익을 기록하며 글로벌 결제 네트워크 사업을 지속했습니다.

keyPoints.text: Mastercard는 전 세계 소비자, 금융기관, 가맹점 및 정부를 연결하는 글로벌 결제 네트워크를 운영하며 전자 결제 솔루션을 제공합니다.
결제 거래의 승인, 청산 및 결제(switch)를 수행하는 독점적인 글로벌 결제 네트워크를 핵심 사업으로 영위합니다.
Mastercard®, Maestro®, Cirrus® 브랜드를 통해 결제 편의성과 효율성을 제고하는 다양한 솔루션을 제공합니다.

riskChanges.text: 결제 산업에 대한 전 세계적인 규제 및 입법 활동 강화가 사업 전반과 운영 결과에 중대한 부정적 영향을 미칠 수 있습니다.
상위 5개 고객사에 대한 매출 집중도가 높으며, 특정 고객과의 관계 종료나 계약 의무 불이행 시 사업에 타격이 발생할 수 있습니다.

businessChanges.text: 인터체인지 수수료 규제로 인해 발행사가 결제 시스템 참여를 줄이거나, 소비자에게 더 높은 수수료를 부과하는 등 제품 경쟁력이 약화될 위험이 있습니다.
경쟁사와의 독점적 또는 준독점적 관계가 고객 이탈을 방지하지 못할 경우 사업 운영에 부정적 영향을 줄 수 있습니다.

financialHighlights.text: 2025 회계연도 매출은 327억 9,100만 달러, 영업이익은 188억 9,700만 달러를 기록했습니다.
순이익은 149억 6,800만 달러이며, 총자산은 541억 5,700만 달러, 현금및현금성자산은 105억 6,600만 달러를 보유하고 있습니다.

watchItems.text: 글로벌 규제 당국의 인터체인지 수수료 압박이 지속될 경우, 수익성 방어와 고객사 유지 전략이 향후 실적의 핵심 변수가 될 것으로 판단됩니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: FY2025 revenue = 327억 9,100만 달러 (USD 32.791B; 32,791 million), period ended 2025-12-31.
FY2025 operating income = 188억 9,700만 달러 (USD 18.897B; 18,897 million), period ended 2025-12-31.
FY2025 net income = 149억 6,800만 달러 (USD 14.968B; 14,968 million), period ended 2025-12-31.
FY2025 total assets = 541억 5,700만 달러 (USD 54.157B; 54,157 million), period ended 2025-12-31.
FY2025 cash and cash equivalents = 105억 6,600만 달러 (USD 10.566B; 10,566 million), period ended 2025-12-31.

If issuers cannot collect or we are required to reduce interchange rates, issuers may be less willing to participate in our four-party payments system. Alternatively, they may reduce the benefits associated with our products, choose to charge higher fees to consumers to attempt to recoup a portion of the costs incurred for their services, or seek a fee reduction from us to decrease the expense of their payment programs (particularly if regulation has a disproportionate impact on us as compared to our competitors in terms of the fees we can charge). These and other impacts could make our products less desirable to consumers, limit our ability to innovate or offer differentiated products, and/or make proprietary three

Our business agreements with these customers may not ultimately reduce the risk (inherent in our business) that customers may terminate their relationships with us in favor of relationships with our competitors, or for other reasons, or might not meet their contractual obligations to us. In addition, a significant portion of our revenue is concentrated among our five largest customers. Loss of business from any of our large customers could have a material adverse impact on our overall business and results of operations. MASTERCARD 2025 FORM 10-K 35 PART I ITEM 1A. RISK FACTORS Exclusive/near exclusive relationships certain customers have with our competitors may have a material adverse impact on our business. While we have exclusive, or nearly-exclusive, relationships with certain of our customers to issue payment products, other customers have similar exclusive, or nearly-exclusive, rel

Overview Mastercard is a technology company in the global payments industry. We connect consumers, financial institutions, merchants, governments, digital partners, businesses and other organizations worldwide by enabling electronic payments and making those payment transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. We make payments easier and more efficient by providing a wide range of payment solutions and services using our family of well-known and trusted brands, including our primary brand Mastercard®, as well as our Maestro® and Cirrus® brands. We operate a payments network that provides choice and flexibility for consumers, merchants and our customers. Through our unique and proprietary global payments network, we switch (authorize, clear and settle) payment transactions.
